title: Edge Cases & Outliers
concept: Edge cases are rare, unusual inputs that differ significantly from typical training examples.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is a spurious correlation?

options.text: An accidental or misleading link between background pixels and target labels that a model exploits
The mathematical fusion of horizontal and vertical CNN layers
A labeling error where text files are corrupted during download

exercises.explain: Spurious correlations are patterns in data (like background snow) that appear related to the label but are not true features.

cards.title: The kangaroo problem
cards.body: When a self-driving car was tested in Australia, it got confused by kangaroos. Why? The car had been trained on millions of miles of roads in Europe and America, where hopping animals don't exist. This is a classic edge case.

cards.title: Edge Case & Outlier
cards.body: An Edge Case is an extreme or unusual operating scenario that a model rarely encounters. An Outlier is an out-of-distribution (OOD) data point that differs significantly from the training distribution.

cards.title: A surprise question
cards.body: Imagine practicing for an addition test using numbers between 1 and 10. The teacher suddenly asks you to solve 999 + 999. It's still addition, but the numbers are far outside the range you practiced. This is an outlier.

cards.title: Unusual Scenarios
cards.body: Examples of vision edge cases: a person walking down the street carrying a large mirror (reflections confuse depth), a cyclist in a dinosaur costume, or a stop sign covered in snow and graffiti.

cards.title: Normal data won't fix it
cards.body: You might think adding another million standard highway images will make a self-driving car safe. But if those million images don't contain the rare edge cases (like kangaroos or flipped trucks), the model's blind spot remains.

cards.title: Distribution Mapping
cards.body: Where edge cases sit relative to standard training data:

left.text:
Standard cars, clear sunny roads.
High model confidence.
Occupies the bulk of training data.

right.text:
Rare events, extreme conditions.
Low confidence / wrong prediction.
Requires targeted search to find.

exercises.prompt: What is an edge case in computer vision?
exercises.explain: Edge cases are unusual, rare scenarios that models struggle to generalize to because they were underrepresented in the training set.

options.text: A rare, unusual input or scenario that differs significantly from typical training examples
The pixel boundary where an object's outline meets the background
A hardware failure where the GPU overheating causes image corruption
A box that has coordinates outside the image frame

exercises.prompt: True or False: Out-of-Distribution (OOD) data matches the exact statistical properties of the training set.
exercises.explain: False. OOD data represents inputs that look significantly different from what the model saw during training.
Unusual visual occurrences like people in costume represent classic edge cases for traffic detectors.
exercises.sentence: A person wearing a dinosaur costume crossing the street is an example of an ___ case.

exercises.prompt: Why are edge cases difficult for standard vision models to handle?
exercises.explain: Because edge cases are rare, standard training sets contain very few examples, leading to model confusion.

options.text: They occur rarely, meaning the model has not seen enough similar examples to generalize
They require more floating-point operations to compute on GPUs
They cannot be annotated using standard bounding boxes

exercises.prompt: You are building a face detection system for an office door. It fails to recognize a worker who is wearing a thick winter mask and sunglasses. What category of failure is this, and how do you address it?

options.text: An edge case caused by heavy occlusion; solve by adding images of people wearing diverse hats, masks, and glasses to the training set.
A hardware sensor failure; solve by replacing the camera with a higher-resolution lens.
A data leakage bug; solve by deleting the testing set files.
An overfitting bug; solve by training for zero epochs.

exercises.explain: Occlusion (face covered) is a classic edge case. Retraining the network with diverse accessories forces it to learn features around the eyes and forehead.
